home.getStarted: Get started with Chat or explore Tutorials to learn more.
home.tagline: Your friendly computer helper. Ask questions, show your screen, and learn at your own pace.

chat.title: Chat with the assistant
chat.body: Type a question in the box at the bottom and press Send. The assistant answers in plain language.

screen.title: Ask about your screen
screen.body: Tell the assistant what you see. It can capture your screen to help answer — just ask about something you're looking at.

voice.title: Talk with your voice
voice.body: Press the microphone button to speak instead of typing. Press it again to stop.

language.title: English or Spanish
language.body: Use the language button in the top right to switch between English and Spanish at any time.

tutorials.title: Step-by-step tutorials
tutorials.body: Open a tutorial to learn things like using Google Search or sending Gmail. Each step guides you on the screen.

help.title: Help is always close
help.body: Press the Help button in the top right to replay this tour whenever you need a reminder.

tutorials.description: Click on a tutorial for a step-by-step interactive lesson on a topic.

intro-welcome.title: Welcome to Granson AI
intro-welcome.text:
This quick tour shows you everything the app can do.

Use the Next button at the bottom to move forward, Back to go to the previous step, or Exit tutorial to stop at any time.

intro-chat.title: Chat with the assistant
intro-chat.text:
This is the chat box. Type any question — in plain words — and press Send or Enter.

You can ask about the computer, the internet, or anything you're curious about.

intro-screen.title: Ask about your screen
intro-screen.text:
You can ask the assistant about what you're looking at. For example, try typing:

• "What is on my screen?"
• "What does this button do?"

The assistant will capture a picture of your screen to help answer.

intro-voice.title: Talk with your voice
intro-voice.text:
Press this microphone button to speak instead of typing.

Press it once to start recording. Press it again when you're done. The assistant will read what you said and reply.

intro-audio-mode.title: Listen to the replies
intro-audio-mode.text:
Turn on Audio mode so the assistant reads its answers out loud.

Press this button once to turn it on. Press it again to turn it off.

intro-language.title: English or Spanish
intro-language.text:
Use this button to switch between English and Spanish.

The whole app — including tutorials and the assistant — changes language right away.

intro-tutorials.title: Step-by-step tutorials
intro-tutorials.text:
Tap any tutorial to start it. Each lesson walks you through a task one step at a time, with highlights on the screen to show you where to click.

Current lessons include Basic Google Search and Gmail.

intro-exit.title: Leaving a tutorial
intro-exit.text:
You can leave any tutorial at any time.

Look for the Exit tutorial button at the bottom-left of the screen while a tutorial is running. Press it and you'll come right back here.

intro-help.title: The Help button
intro-help.text:
This Help button in the top right of the panel is always here.

Press it whenever you want to see this tour again — you won't lose your place in the app.

intro-finish.title: You're ready to go
intro-finish.text:
That's it! Press Finish to start using Granson AI.

Remember: the Help button is always there if you need a reminder.

gs-intro.title: Lesson 3: Basic Google Search
gs-intro.body:
An introduction to using the internet.

What you will learn today:
• Terms
• Browser Layout
• Navigating the Internet
• Using Google Chrome
• Searching for Websites

gs-internet.title: The Internet
gs-internet.body: A global computer network for information and communication—many connected networks using shared rules (protocols).

gs-www.title: World Wide Web (WWW)
gs-www.body: The Web: a system for reaching documents and other pages over the Internet.

gs-search-engine.title: Search engine
gs-search-engine.body: A service that lets you search for content on the World Wide Web (WWW).

gs-websites.title: Websites
gs-websites.body: Related web pages under one domain name, usually from one person or organization.

gs-url.title: Web address / URL
gs-url.body: Tells where a page lives on the Web. There are four parts to a URL.

gs-browser-layout.title: Browser layout
gs-browser-layout.body: Next: the main parts of the Chrome window—follow the highlights.

gs-tabs.title: Tabs
gs-tabs.body:
A tab is another page at the top of the window.

Click the + next to your tabs to open a new tab.

gs-back-forward-refresh.title: Back, Forward, Refresh
gs-back-forward-refresh.body:
Back / Forward: pages you already visited.
Refresh: reload this page.

Find these left of the address bar. After you open a link, try Back.

gs-address-bar.title: Address bar
gs-address-bar.body:
Shows the page’s address.

Click it, type an address (try wikipedia.org), press Enter.

gs-open-tabs-recent.title: Open tabs / Recently closed
gs-open-tabs-recent.body:
Reopen a tab you closed by mistake.

Use the tab search or list control in the tab row (or right‑click a tab).

gs-share-page.title: Share page
gs-share-page.body:
Send this page as a link.

Click the Share icon (near the address bar).

gs-bookmarks.title: Add / remove bookmarks
gs-bookmarks.body:
Save this page to find it again.

Click the star on the right of the address bar.

gs-chrome-user.title: Chrome user
gs-chrome-user.body:
Your Google account.

Click your picture or initial (top right).

gs-chrome-menu.title: Chrome menu
gs-chrome-menu.body:
More tools and settings.

Click the three dots ⋮ (top right).

gs-search-bar.title: Search bar
gs-search-bar.body:
Use when you don’t know the exact address—or you have a question.

Type in the top field or Google’s box, press Enter. Try Images or Videos in the results.

gs-try-layout.title: Let’s try it out
gs-try-layout.body: You’ve seen the layout. Next: open Chrome and go somewhere.

gs-navigating.title: Navigating the Internet
gs-navigating.body: Open Chrome, then type a site you know in the address bar.

gs-open-chrome.title: Open Google Chrome
gs-open-chrome.body: Double‑click the Chrome icon (desktop, taskbar, Start menu, or Dock).

gs-navigate-url.title: Go to a website
gs-navigate-url.body: Click the address bar, type a site, press Enter. Use Back when you’re done.

gs-try-again.title: Let’s try it out
gs-try-again.body: Next: practice search when you don’t have the exact address.

gs-searching-intro.title: Searching for websites
gs-searching-intro.body: Use search for a topic or question—not only when you know the URL.

gs-search-practice.title: Try a search
gs-search-practice.body: Click the search/address field, type a short question or topic, press Enter. Open one result.

gs-more-options.title: More options
gs-more-options.body: Extra tools live under ⋮ and your profile icon.

gs-review.title: Let’s review
gs-review.body:
Think through:
1. Where are favorite saved pages?
2. What does WWW mean?
3. Where can you find videos in search?
4. How do you save an image from Image results?
5. Name a popular search engine.
6. How do categories help on a website?
7. How might you find a place’s location?

gs-next-time.title: Next time
gs-next-time.body: We’ll learn more about staying safe on the Internet.

gm-01-intro.title: Welcome to Lesson 6: Intro to Gmail
gm-01-intro.text:
Welcome! This lesson is an Introduction on how to use Email (Gmail).

What You Will Learn Today:
• What's an Email?
• Signing up for a Gmail account
• Logging into Gmail
• Opening and Replying to Emails
• Sending Emails
• Practicing Sending Emails

gm-02-what-is-email.title: What's an Email?
gm-02-what-is-email.text:
Sending an email is just like mailing someone a letter at the post office.

• The letter is mailed digitally through the Internet, and the person receives the letter instantly.
• You can include pictures, documents, videos & other computer files.
• You can send an email to one person, or to multiple people at the same time.
• You can access your email on any device that has the internet.
• It's FREE!

gm-03-email-parts-labels.title: Parts of an Email Address
gm-03-email-parts-labels.text:
Example: jeanpierreellameh@computers4people.org

• Like a real address, the email address has to be typed correctly, or the other person will not receive their mail.

• It will always have two main parts, which are connected by the "@" symbol.

gm-04-email-parts-rules.title: Parts of an Email Address
gm-04-email-parts-rules.text:
Example: jeanpierreellameh@computers4people.org

• The first part of the email address is the person's username (here, jeanpierreellameh).

• This is similar to the name you write on a regular mailing address.

gm-05-email-username.title: Parts of an Email Address
gm-05-email-username.text:
Example: jeanpierreellameh@computers4people.org

• The second part of the email address is the location (here, computers4people).

• It could be the name of an email provider, the organization the person works for, or a website that the person is associated with.

gm-08-open-chrome.title: Signing up for a Gmail Account
gm-08-open-chrome.text:
Let's sign up for a Gmail account!

First, open Google Chrome on your computer.

In the address bar at the top, type: www.gmail.com and press Enter.

You will see the Gmail sign-in page.

gm-09-create-account.title: Signing up for a Gmail Account
gm-09-create-account.text:
Click "Create account" next to the sign-in box.

A form will appear asking for your information.

gm-10-enter-name.title: Signing up for a Gmail Account
gm-10-enter-name.text:
Enter your first and last name in the fields provided.

Then click "Next" to continue.

gm-11-choose-email.title: Signing up for a Gmail Account
gm-11-choose-email.text:
Choose your Gmail address. This will be your username.

• Usernames are unique to each account, and it may be hard to find something that is not already taken.

• Use something appropriate to share with anyone you may give your email to.

Then click "Next" to continue.

gm-12-choose-password.title: Signing up for a Gmail Account
gm-12-choose-password.text:
Create a password for your account.

• Your password must be at least 8 characters. Use a mix of letters, numbers, and symbols to make it strong.

• Do not use your name or other common words.

Type your password again to confirm it, then click "Next".

gm-12a-personal-info.title: Signing up for a Gmail Account
gm-12a-personal-info.text:
Google will now ask for some personal information.

You may enter a phone number and recovery email address. These are optional, but help keep your account secure.

Enter your birthday using the Month, Day, and Year fields.

Select your gender from the dropdown menu.

When you are done, click "Next" to continue.

gm-12b-verify-phone.title: Signing up for a Gmail Account
gm-12b-verify-phone.text:
Google will send a 6-digit verification code to your phone number as a text message.

Check your phone for the text message from Google.

Type the 6-digit code into the "Enter verification code" field.

Then click "Verify" to continue.

gm-13-logging-in.title: Logging into Gmail
gm-13-logging-in.text: Let's log into your Gmail account!

gm-14-go-to-google.title: Logging into Gmail
gm-14-go-to-google.text:
Open Google Chrome and go to www.google.com.

In the top right corner, click "Gmail".

gm-15-enter-email.title: Logging into Gmail
gm-15-enter-email.text:
You will see a Sign In page.

Type your Gmail address into the "Email or phone" field.

Then click "Next".

gm-16-enter-password.title: Logging into Gmail
gm-16-enter-password.text:
Type your password into the "Enter your password" field.

Then click "Next".

gm-17-inbox.title: Logging into Gmail
gm-17-inbox.text:
You are now logged in!

This is your Gmail inbox. Here you can see your emails, compose new ones, and navigate your mail folders on the left side.

gm-18-opening-replying.title: Opening and Replying to Email
gm-18-opening-replying.text: Now let's open and reply to an email!

gm-19-open-inbox.title: Opening and Replying to Email
gm-19-open-inbox.text:
Click on "Inbox" on the left side of the screen.

You will see a list of emails you have received. Click on an email to open it.

gm-20-read-email.title: Opening and Replying to Email
gm-20-read-email.text:
The email is now open and you can read the full message.

At the bottom of the email you will see a "Reply" button and a "Forward" button.

gm-21-click-reply.title: Opening and Replying to Email
gm-21-click-reply.text: To reply, click the "Reply" button at the bottom of the email, or the reply arrow icon in the top right corner of the message.

gm-22-type-reply.title: Opening and Replying to Email
gm-22-type-reply.text:
A text box will appear at the bottom of the screen.

Type your reply message in the text box.

When you are done, click the "Send" button to send your reply.

gm-23-reply-sent.title: Opening and Replying to Email
gm-23-reply-sent.text:
Your reply has been sent!

You will see your sent message appear in the email thread, and a "Message sent" notification at the bottom of the screen.

gm-25-click-compose.title: Sending Email
gm-25-click-compose.text: Click the "Compose" button in the top left corner of your inbox.

gm-26-new-message.title: Sending Email
gm-26-new-message.text:
A "New Message" window will appear in the bottom right corner of your screen.

Click on the "To" field and type the email address of the person you want to send the email to.

gm-27-enter-recipient.title: Sending Email
gm-27-enter-recipient.text:
Type the email address of the person you are sending the email to and press Enter.

Their name will appear in the "To" field.

gm-28-type-message.title: Sending Email
gm-28-type-message.text:
Click on the "Subject" field and type a short description of your email.

Then click in the large area below and type your message.

gm-29-message-sent.title: Sending Email
gm-29-message-sent.text:
When you are done, click the "Send" button.

Your email has been sent! You will see a "Message sent" notification appear in the bottom left corner of the screen.

tutorial.visualBadgeHint: Lesson format: text only, screen demo, or both
tutorial.highlightErrorHint: Make sure Chrome is visible, then try Next again.

chat.heading: Chat
chat.description: Ask any question about your computer here. The assistant can capture your screen automatically when it helps answer.
chat.screenCaptureFailed: I tried to view your screen but could not capture it (grant screen permission in System Settings, or use the desktop app). Describe what you see instead.
chat.finishScreenFailed: Sorry, I could not finish analyzing your screen.
chat.emptyResponse: I could not generate a response. Please try again.
chat.unexpectedResponse: Sorry, there was an unexpected response from the assistant.

streamErrors.invalidCapture: Invalid response while preparing screen capture.

chat.audioFailedDescription: Check that OPENAI_API_KEY is set for text-to-speech, or try toggling audio mode off and on after clicking the app.

dashboard.heading: Dashboard
dashboard.description: This is a protected route accessible only to signed-in users.
dashboard.notConfigured: Sign-in is not set up yet (Supabase URL and key are missing). Add NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY to your .env file to enable the dashboard.

errors.supabaseNotConfigured: Sign-in is not configured. You cannot save profile changes until Supabase environment variables are set.

settings.heading: Settings
settings.description: Manage your account and profile settings.

options.heading: Options
options.description: Change how the app looks and runs.

profileForm.heading: Profile
profileForm.siteDescription: This is how others will see you on the site.
profileForm.usernameDescription: This is your public display name. It can be your real name or a pseudonym.
profileForm.emailDescription: This is your verified email address.
profileForm.bioPlaceholder: Tell us a little bit about yourself
profileForm.validationUsernameMin: Username must be at least 2 characters.
profileForm.validationUsernameMax: Username must not be longer than 30 characters.
profileForm.validationBioMax: Biography cannot be longer than 160 characters.
